title: MFA-secret lifecycle — enrolment through erasure
description: Reference data illustrating the state transitions of Identity.mfa_secret_encrypted. Every transition is guarded by the observer + fires the corresponding event. The plaintext TOTP secret NEVER touches durable storage — it exists only in memory during enrolment preview and in the KMS-decrypted form during verification.

steps: MfaSecretEncryptor::encrypt(plaintext_secret) generates a KMS data key + AES-256-GCM encrypts
Ciphertext blob persisted to Identity.mfa_secret_encrypted
10 recovery codes generated + bcrypt-hashed + persisted to Identity.mfa_recovery_codes_hashed
Fires MfaEnrolled event
Plaintext secret + recovery codes shown once in the API response; discarded after
MfaSecretEncryptor::decrypt returns plaintext under the previous KMS key
MfaSecretEncryptor::encrypt re-encrypts under the current key
Ciphertext blob updated in-place
NO event fired — this is a cryptographic maintenance operation, not a lifecycle event
MfaSecretEncryptor::decrypt returns plaintext
TOTP algorithm computes the expected 6-digit code for now() and now() +/- 30s (drift tolerance)
Constant-time comparison against submitted code
On success: Sanctum PAT issued, plaintext secret zeroed in memory
On failure: failed_attempts_count incremented, plaintext secret zeroed
state_transitions.notes: The ciphertext stays unchanged — verification is a read-only operation on the encrypted blob.

state_transitions.trigger: POST /api/v1/auth/mfa/disable OR admin action OR account recovery
steps: Requires re-authentication (fresh password prompt within the last 5 minutes)
Identity.mfa_secret_encrypted ← NULL
Identity.mfa_recovery_codes_hashed ← NULL (purged in same transaction)
Fires MfaDisabled event
10 fresh recovery codes generated
Old bcrypt-hashed codes purged; new codes bcrypt-hashed and persisted
Fires RecoveryCodesRegenerated event
New plaintext codes shown once in the response
state_transitions.notes: The TOTP secret itself is unchanged. Only the recovery-code array is rotated.

steps: Identity row hard-deleted
Ciphertext blob is gone at rest
KMS data key CANNOT be re-derived (KMS-side purge follows through envelope-encryption's design guarantee)

invariants: The plaintext TOTP secret is generated server-side + returned ONCE in the enrolment-preview response. Client re-submits the confirmation code to prove they scanned the QR/entered the URI; server persists the encrypted form only after that proof.
Individual recovery codes are single-use — consumed codes are removed from the JSONB array in the same transaction as the login. Re-submitting a consumed code returns RECOVERY_CODE_INVALID.

MFA secret + recovery codes are ATOMICALLY paired in the DB — either both present (enrolled) or both null (not enrolled). A row with only one populated is a compliance failure caught by SchemaCurrentProbe.
The KMS data-key ciphertext travels inside the mfa_secret_encrypted blob; the plaintext data key lives only in a short-lived in-memory cache (see caches.json `idn:kms_data_key:current`).
